Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x4000 "BACKTOREALITY-C000-4000" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 jsr $c000 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Back to Reality.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added a routine at $C000 to display the loading screen and wait for any key or the fire button on either joystick to enter the game.
Once the loading screen has been displayed, the routine then initialises colour RAM for the game itself.
Also made the following changes to allow the fire button in port 2 start the game:
1. Patched the JSR $FFE4 instruction at $4F87 to be JSR $C100 instead 2. Added a small routine at $C100 to check the fire button in port 2 - if pressed, then it sets the accumulator to $20 and returns, which causes the game to start just as if the space bar was pressed. If not pressed, the routine then JMPs to $FFE4 to carry on the normal code flow.
Also zeroed out the following memory ranges as they contained an unnecessary repeating memory block:
$0880 - $0BFF $1800 - $1FFF $C000 - $CFFF
